title: Type annotations needed
explanation: This error occurs when the compiler cannot infer the type of a value and needs you to provide an explicit type annotation. This commonly happens with methods like parse(), collect(), or when using generic types.

fixes.description: Add a type annotation to the variable
fixes.code:
let x: i32 = "42".parse().unwrap();
let v: Vec<String> = Vec::new();

fixes.description: Use the turbofish syntax on the method

tip: The turbofish syntax (::<Type>) lets you specify types on method calls. It is called turbofish because ::<> looks like a fish.
